qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SMTP header injection via bare CR in HELO/EHLO host argument
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes header injection via bare CR in HELO/EHLO commands affecting the Received header generation. The advisory describes a completely different vulnerability: prototype pollution DoS caused by sending '__proto__' as an email header name, which crashes the header parser at haraka-email-message/lib/header.js:215-218. The vulnerable code locations, root cause, and attack vectors are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a prototype pollution DoS in Haraka's email header parser (haraka-email-message/lib/header.js:215-218) where sending '__proto__' as a header name causes Object.prototype to be returned instead of undefined, leading to a TypeError crash when attempting to call .push() on it. The qualifying finding smtp_header_injection_via_helo describes a different vulnerability: bare CR injection in HELO/EHLO commands affecting Received header generation in connection.js. This finding does not involve the header parser's __proto__ handling or the _add_header function described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: header parser at haraka-email-message/lib/header.js:215-218 treats '__proto__' as a property access returning Object.prototype instead of undefined
Advisory impact: TypeError crash when calling Object.prototype.push(), triggering process.exit(1) via uncaughtException handler
Finding smtp_header_injection_via_helo: Different vulnerability in connection.js involving bare CR injection in HELO/EHLO command processing
No finding mentions haraka-email-message/lib/header.js, the _add_header function, or __proto__ header handling
